name: Insider Threat: Privileged Admin
description: Attack tree modelling malicious actions by a trusted privileged administrator abusing legitimate access for data theft, sabotage, or espionage
root_objective: Abuse privileged admin access for data exfiltration or sabotage

nodes.title: Insider Abuse of Privileged Access
nodes.description: A trusted administrator uses legitimate access to exfiltrate sensitive data, sabotage systems, or enable external adversaries

nodes.title: Exfiltrate Sensitive Data
nodes.description: Copy intellectual property, customer data, or trade secrets using legitimate admin access

nodes.title: Bulk Database Export
nodes.description: Export production database contents using legitimate DBA credentials to a personal device or cloud storage

nodes.title: Email/Cloud Drive Exfiltration
nodes.description: Send files to personal email or sync to personal cloud storage using sanctioned collaboration tools

nodes.title: Photograph Screens
nodes.description: Use a personal phone camera to photograph sensitive data displayed on screen, bypassing DLP controls

nodes.title: Create Persistent Backdoor Access
nodes.description: Establish hidden access mechanisms that persist after employment ends

nodes.title: Create Hidden Admin Account
nodes.description: Create a backdoor user account or service principal with admin rights that is not tied to the insider's identity

nodes.title: Install SSH Keys on Servers
nodes.description: Add personal SSH public keys to production servers' authorized_keys files

nodes.title: Sabotage Critical Systems
nodes.description: Deliberately damage or disrupt production systems using admin credentials

nodes.title: Delete Production Data/Backups
nodes.description: Wipe production databases and destroy backup copies to cause maximum business disruption

nodes.title: Modify Security Tool Configuration
nodes.description: Disable or misconfigure security monitoring tools to create blind spots for future attacks

nodes.title: No Privileged Access Monitoring (PAM)
nodes.description: Organisation lacks privileged access management, session recording, or anomaly detection for admin actions

nodes.title: Excessive Standing Privileges
nodes.description: Administrators have permanent unrestricted access rather than just-in-time, just-enough privilege
